description: Persist a pending-blocked state and add the resolve-blocked / update --continue commit op — closes FGAP-080. When resyncSchema blocks, persist what the rollback currently discards (target schema body/version, the migration chain, the per-item failure set from TASK-048's diagnostic), and add a commit op mirroring resolve-conflict: re-validate the corrected block against the target schema, on success write the new schema via writeSchemaCheckedForDir and advance the merge base via stampBaselineFromBody — so the schema converges and stops re-blocking. The diagnose -> fix -> retry loop, end-to-end, matching the conflicts/resolve-conflict model.
acceptance_criteria: A blocked update persists a pending-blocked record (target schema version/body reference, migration chain, per-item failure set); the schema/block/migrations.json byte-unchanged contract is preserved (the record lives outside them).

After the operator fixes the items or widens the local schema, ONE command (resolve-blocked or update --continue) re-validates the block against the target schema; on pass it writes the new schema and advances the merge base so a subsequent update reports in-sync (no re-block); on fail it reports the remaining per-item failures and writes nothing.
The flow mirrors resolve-conflict end-to-end: surfaced detail -> operator fix -> commit op -> base advance; demonstrated on a seeded blocked substrate through to convergence.
Per-schema rollback and per-component application (DEC-0018) unchanged for schemas not being resolved.
build + check + full test green; parity-check exit 0; fresh adversarial probe.
notes: Closes FGAP-080; advances STORY-006/011/012/014. Depends on TASK-048 (the persisted failure set is its diagnostic). Reuses stampBaselineFromBody, writeSchemaCheckedForDir, validateBlockWithMigrationForDir, the object store.
